---
title: "Research"
---

::: {.page-icon}
<img src="image/research.svg" alt="" aria-hidden="true">
:::

# Welcome to my engine room

Every world needs something that keeps it running. This is where I develop research ideas, build studies, analyse data, and learn new methods. My work draws on quantitative, qualitative, and computational approaches to explore questions in marketing, management, tourism, and artificial intelligence.


## Field experiments

One project involved a field experiment testing whether adding human presence to short videos could increase embodied mental simulation and encourage click-through behaviour.

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Human-presence**
<iframe width="100%" height="315"
src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/YC0EvhrqDv4"
title="Human Presence"
frameborder="0"
allowfullscreen>
</iframe>
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Human-absence**
<iframe width="100%" height="315"
src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/1uKujcBwDbk"
title="Human Absence"
frameborder="0"
allowfullscreen>
</iframe>
:::

::::

## GenAI-generated videos

Not everything is data and code. Some of my projects involve creating AI-generated video stimuli, such as this example of an AI-driven supervision system.

<iframe width="100%" height="315"
src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/roiCt3d-7IU"
title="AI supervision system"
frameborder="0"
allowfullscreen>
</iframe>

## RStudio

R is one of the most frequently used tools. I use it to analyse experimental data, estimate Bayesian networks, build predictive models, and apply techniques such as Elastic Net and Random Forest. Below are the methods I am proficient in.

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(plotly)

scores <- data.frame(
  Category = c("Between-subjects experiments", "PLS-SEM", "Machine-learning", "In-depth interview", "Systematic literature review"),
  Value = c(6, 4, 4, 2, 1)
)

plot_ly(
  type = 'scatterpolar',
  r = scores$Value,
  theta = scores$Category,
  fill = 'toself',
  name = "Number of papers",
  line = list(color = '#1f3a93'),
  fillcolor = 'rgba(255,214,0,0.4)'
) %>%
  layout(
    polar = list(
      radialaxis = list(visible = TRUE, range = c(0,10))
    ),
    showlegend = TRUE
  )
```
